claims.claim: Twilio terminates PSTN and bridges to runtime over WebSocket carrying base64 mu-law (deepgram-ebook line 706-722)
claims.found: lines 707-721 'provider such as Twilio. The provider terminates the PSTN call and establishes a bidirectional media stream, commonly via WebSocket'; lines 716-721 'mu-law or A-law PCM ... Deepgram speech models accept mu-law PCM natively'. (Note: source says mu-law over WebSocket but does not literally use the word 'base64'; base64 is the actual Twilio Media Streams encoding, corroborated by twilio.py serializer.)

claims.claim: Deepgram notes WebSocket concurrency limits are a primary scaling constraint for telephony (ebook line 764)
claims.found: lines 759-766 'Scaling and Concurrency ... Scaling challenges typically arise in: WebSocket concurrency limits, Orchestrator throughput, Downstream LLM rate limits'

summary: All 3 code_refs and all 6 source figures verified against ground truth. No WRONG or MISCITED. Two minor non-blocking notes: 'Opus-bound' frames are 16-bit PCM at the cited line (Opus is applied downstream by aiortc), and the deepgram source says mu-law over WebSocket without literally saying 'base64' (though base64 is the correct Twilio encoding).
